_comment: HAND-AUTHORED falsifiability scenarios (placement-grounded SYNTHETIC outcome records, NOT telemetry and NOT generated from the estate graph — generating them from the graph would rig the negative-control test, INV-22). Each 'observed' set is a hand-picked SUBSET of the hosts that would truly be hit (verifiable against real Proxmox placement / LibreNMS dependency config) plus one SURPRISE host that genuinely lives elsewhere and is not transitively in the target's radius. The negative control is SIBLINGS-SYMMETRIC (a same-shape null, TG-87): the real prediction AND its degree-preserving shuffle both include common-cause siblings — no rigged asymmetry. Under that honest null, STRICT falsifiability (expect_falsifiable=true) holds only on HIGH-FANOUT placement nodes: pve01 (59 guests) and pve04 (27 guests) beat the shuffle (control_tp=0), because with dozens of siblings a degree-preserving shuffle rarely reproduces the specific observed subset. pve02 (only 7 guests) carries expect_falsifiable=false: too few siblings, so the shuffle reproduces the observed 3 (control_tp==real_tp==3) — the SAME underpowered-null limitation as the depends_on scenarios (redis03/sw01, 11-edge tier). So 3 of 5 scenarios are documented underpowered-null limitations and 2 genuinely beat their same-shape control; the AGGREGATE differentiator still holds (AvgRealTP 2.4 vs AvgControlTP 1.2, SignalRatio 2.0 > 1). Measured limitation (fixture 2026-07-17; siblings-symmetric-control recalibration TG-87, 2026-07-19) — revisit as more high-fanout tiers and the LibreNMS dependency/learned tiers populate more edges. Placement facts used: pve02 hosts gitea01/redis02/meshcentral01 (7 guests); pve04 hosts cap01/claude01/dmz01 (27 guests); pve01 hosts cl01file01/code01/cloudbeaver01 (59 guests); nextcloud01 declares redis03 a dependency parent; ap01/ap03 declare sw01 their dependency parent.
